hero_title: Preserve Audit Evidence for AML Cases
hero_subtitle: SFTY connects provider outputs into a structured compliance workflow that preserves audit trails and links escalation decisions with case evidence for digital asset AML review.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Audit readiness weakens across scattered case evidence
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to maintain a clear and traceable audit trail when AML case escalation relies on disconnected provider outputs and manual handoffs. This fragmentation slows review processes and complicates defensible documentation of escalation decisions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY links provider checks, case reviews, decision logs and evidence into one structured workflow to help compliance teams maintain clear, traceable and defensible audit trails for AML case escalation.
workflow_title: From alerts to case review and evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes AML alerts through structured reviews, case creation, escalation and decision logging while preserving detailed audit evidence for compliance teams.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond case chaos. Standardized workflow control
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented AML alerts and provider outputs into structured cases with linked decisions and audit-ready evidence for clearer escalation and compliance oversight.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY gives VASPs a controlled compliance layer to preserve audit evidence, govern provider outputs, and support bank due diligence with secure enterprise deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how providers’ data connect to audit-ready evidence

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer that connects screening, monitoring, alerts, and case management into a connected AML workflow for digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support AML case escalation?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes alerts and provider outputs into structured case workflows with decision logs, escalation rules, and evidence preservation for efficient AML case escalation.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, reviewer decisions, evidence packages, and audit logs to support review readiness and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY connects with existing KYC, KYT, Travel Rule, and screening providers without replacing them, consolidating their outputs into a connected compliance layer.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace AML screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY works above AML screening providers by structuring their checks and alerts into operational workflows and audit-ready cases.
final_title: Preserve audit-ready evidence for AML case escalation
final_description: Review how SFTY connects case decisions, provider outputs and evidence into a controlled audit trail for VASP compliance workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
